zh.lifeSummary: 當Amy Jane Diffee於1849年11月6日出生在美國阿肯色州時，她的父親Jonathan D. Diffee25歲，母親Mary Ann Shuffield22歲。她於1874年9月3日與William Marion Hughes在美國阿肯色州克拉克結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她於1880年住在美國阿肯色州霍特 斯普林德 洛奇且於1900年住在美國阿肯色州霍特 斯普林亨德森 堂史普。她去世於1905年12月8日在美國阿肯色州霍特 斯普林俾斯麥享年56歲，埋葬在美國阿肯色州霍特 斯普林俾斯麥科華士 希爾 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Amy Jane Diffee于1849年11月6日出生在美国阿肯色州时，她的父亲Jonathan D. Diffee25岁，母亲Mary Ann Shuffield22岁。她于1874年9月3日与William Marion Hughes在美国阿肯色州克拉克结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她于1880年住在美国阿肯色州霍特 斯普林德 洛奇且于1900年住在美国阿肯色州霍特 斯普林亨德森 堂史普。她去世于1905年12月8日在美国阿肯色州霍特 斯普林俾斯麥享年56岁，埋葬在美国阿肯色州霍特 斯普林俾斯麥科華士 希爾 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Diffee
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Diffey  . Compare   Defee  .

History: William Diffee, from whom present-day American bearers of this name are descended, was born c. 1710, probably in Ireland, and died in 1790 in Randolph County, NC. His surname was also spelled Diffey, while his descendants also bear the surname Defee .

variants.name: Diffey
meanings: English (Dorset): perhaps a nickname from Anglo-Norman French defaé, defieu ‘faithless, disloyal’. Compare Diffee .

variants.name: Defee
meanings: History: This surname is borne by the descendants of William Diffee, born c. 1710, probably in Ireland, died 1790 in Randolph County, NC. The same spelling is recorded in London, England, in the 18th century.

nameMeanings.name: Amy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimie
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

variants.name: Aimée
meanings: originally a vernacular nickname meaning ‘beloved’, from the past participle of French aimer ‘to love’ (Latin amare; compare Amy ). It has been in use in French since the Middle Ages, although it has never been very common. It is now also used, with or without the accent, as a given name in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Amie
meanings: Variant spelling of   Amy  , corresponding in form to the French vocabulary word meaning ‘(female) friend’.

variants.name: Ami
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries -  1859: Clark, Arkansas, United States 1873: Hot Spring, Arkansas, United States
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

historicEvents.title: Skirmish at Benton
historicEvents.description: Confederate forces in Arkansas began an invasion of Missouri, while other Confederate sources probed the line around Little Rock. On July  6, 1864 the fourth Arkansas Cavalry tried to break the line around Little Rock one soldier was killed, eight were wounded, three went missing from the Union side and four were killed and six wounded from the Confederate side.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Encyclopedia of Arkansas History & Culture-Skirmish at Benton

historicEvents.description: Abraham Lincoln is assassinated by John Wilkes Booth.

historicEvents.title: The Assassination of James Garfield
historicEvents.description: Garfield was shot twice by Charles J. Guitea at Railroad Station in Washington, D.C. on July 2, 1881. After eleven weeks of intensive and other care Garfield died in Elberon, New Jersey, the second of four presidents to be assassinated, following Abraham Lincoln.

historicEvents.title: The Mosaic Templar is Founded
historicEvents.description: The Mosaic Templar is an African American fraternal organization founded in Little Rock. it was founded by former slaves, John Edward Bush and Chester W. Keatts. It was part of a movement that was going on at the time, where everyone was forming fraternities and sororities. The main departments for this one where endowment, monument, analysis, uniform, rank, recapitulation, records, and a juvenile division.
historicEvents.urlTitle: The Encyclopedia of Arkansas History & Culture- Mosaic Templars of America

historicEvents.title: Plessy vs. Ferguson
historicEvents.description: A landmark decision of the U.S. Supreme Court upholding the constitutionality of racial segregation laws for public facilities if the segregated facilities were equal in quality. It's widely regarded as one of the worst decisions in U.S. Supreme Court history.

historicEvents.title: William H. Fuller Grows 70 Acres of Rice
historicEvents.description: Rice is one Arkansas leading crops, in 1904 William H. Fuller planted 70 acres of rice, this act is what started the making rice the leading crop in Arkansas.
historicEvents.urlTitle: The Encyclopedia of Arkansas History & Culture- Rice Industry

en.lifeSummary: When Amy Jane Diffee was born on 6 November 1849, in Arkansas, United States, her father, Jonathan D. Diffee, was 25 and her mother, Mary Ann Shuffield, was 22. She married William Marion Hughes on 3 September 1874, in Clark, Arkansas, United States. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 2 daughters. She lived in De Roche, Hot Spring, Arkansas, United States in 1880 and Henderson Township, Hot Spring, Arkansas, United States in 1900. She died on 8 December 1905, in Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arkansas, United States, at the age of 56, and was buried in Forest Hill Cemetery, Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arkansas, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Amy Jane Diffee est née le 6 novembre 1849, à Arkansas, États-Unis, son père, Jonathan D. Diffee, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mary Ann Shuffield, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé William Marion Hughes le 3 septembre 1874, à Clark, Arkansas, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 2 filles. Elle a vécu à De Roche, Hot Spring, Arkansas, États-Unis en 1880 et à Henderson Township, Hot Spring, Arkansas, États-Unis en 1900. Elle est décédée le 8 décembre 1905, à Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arkansas, États-Unis, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterrée à Forest Hill Cemetery, Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arkansas, États-Unis.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Amy Jane Diffee è nata il il 6 novembre 1849 nel Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America, sua padre, Jonathan D. Diffee, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Mary Ann Shuffield, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato William Marion Hughes il 3 settembre 1874, nel Clark, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 2 figlie. Vissuto a De Roche, Hot Spring, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1880 e nel Henderson Township, Hot Spring, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1900. Morì il 8 dicembre 1905, nel Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolta a Forest Hill Cemetery, Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Amy Jane Diffeeが1849年11月6日にアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州で生まれた時，父Jonathan D. Diffeeは25歳，母Mary Ann Shuffieldは22歳であった。1874年9月3日にアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州クラークでWilliam Marion Hughesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1880年にはアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ホート スプリングド ロチェに，1900年にはアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ホート スプリングヘンダーソン トウンシュイップに住んでいた。彼女は1905年12月8日にアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ホート スプリングビスマルックで56歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ホート スプリングビスマルックフォーエスト ヒル セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Amy Jane Diffee(이)가 1849년 11월 6일 미국 아칸소 주에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Jonathan D. Diffee(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Ann Shuffield(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1874년 9월 3일 미국 아칸소 주 클라르크에서 William Marion Hughes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1880년도에 미국 아칸소 주 호트 스플링 데 록헤에서, 1900년도에 미국 아칸소 주 호트 스플링 헨더슨 타운시프에서 살았다. 그녀는 1905년 12월 8일 미국 아칸소 주 호트 스플링 비스마크에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 아칸소 주 호트 스플링 비스마크 포레스트 힐 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Amy Jane Diffee nasceu em 6 de novembro de 1849, em Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Jonathan D. Diffee, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mary Ann Shuffield, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com William Marion Hughes em 3 de setembro de 1874, em Clark, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela viveu em De Roche, Hot Spring, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América em 1880 e em Henderson Township, Hot Spring, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América em 1900. Ela faleceu em 8 de dezembro de 1905, em Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América, com 56 anos, e foi sepultada em Forest Hill Cemetery, Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Amy Jane Diffee родилась 6 ноября 1849 г. в местности Арканзас, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Jonathan D. Diffee было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Ann Shuffield, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Marion Hughes 3 сентября 1874 г. в местности кларк, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она жила в местности де рош, хот спринг, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в 1880 году и в местности хендирсон тоуншип, хот спринг, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в 1900 году. Она умерла 8 декабря 1905 г. в местности бисмаркк, хот спринг, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 56 лет и была похоронена в местности форест хилл кеметири, бисмаркк, хот спринг, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Amy Jane Diffee nació el 6 de noviembre de 1849, en Arkansas, Estados Unidos, su papá, Jonathan D. Diffee, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Mary Ann Shuffield, tenía 22 años. Se casó con William Marion Hughes el 3 de septiembre de 1874, en Clark, Arkansas, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 2 hijas. Vivió en De Roche, Hot Spring, Arkansas, Estados Unidos en 1880 y en Henderson Township, Hot Spring, Arkansas, Estados Unidos en 1900. Murió el 8 de diciembre de 1905, en Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arkansas, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultada en Forest Hill Cemetery, Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arkansas, Estados Unidos.
